title: Do Deep Learning Models Keep a Privacy Signature? Auditing Differential Privacy from Weights
description_es: Este trabajo investiga si los modelos de aprendizaje profundo entrenados con privacidad diferencial conservan una firma detectable en sus pesos que permita auditar el nivel de privacidad aplicado.
abstract: Differential privacy provides theoretical guarantees but verifying its application post-training remains challenging. This paper investigates whether deep learning models trained with differential privacy retain an identifiable signature in their weight distributions. We propose methods for auditing DP compliance from model weights alone, enabling verification of privacy claims without access to training data or process.

citations.apa: Rodríguez-Barroso, N., Jiménez-López, D., Luzón, M. V., & Herrera, F. (2026). Do Deep Learning Models Keep a Privacy Signature? Auditing Differential Privacy from Weights. IEEE Computational Intelligence Magazine.
citations.mla: Rodríguez-Barroso, Nuria, D. Jiménez-López, María Victoria Luzón, and Francisco Herrera. "Do Deep Learning Models Keep a Privacy Signature? Auditing Differential Privacy from Weights." IEEE Computational Intelligence Magazine, 2026.
